query_normalized: evaluating the use of graph neural networks for anomaly detection in network traffic computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) as it investigates the same core mechanism (Graph Neural Networks for Anomaly Detection) and measures the same dependent variable (detection performance), even though it evaluates on different graph populations (social/citation networks) rather than the specific network traffic domain requested. It provides necessary methodological context for a literature review on GNN-based anomaly detection capabilities.
